topic: Self-Hosted AI Coding Assistants 2026: Tabby vs Continue + Ollama vs Void

key_points: Comprehensive 2026 deployment guide for Tabby + Qwen2.5-Coder on GPU cloud
Cost breakdown: single A100 80GB = $749/month; break-even vs Copilot at 39 developers
HumanEval benchmarks: Qwen2.5-Coder 32B = 92.7% vs GitHub Copilot ~75% estimated
TTFT comparison: Tabby+Qwen32B 150-400ms vs Copilot 80-150ms
FauxPilot declared effectively abandoned as of 2026
Defines three distinct flavors: local-first data, local model execution, and self-hosted
'Supports Ollama' is now table-stakes marketing — no longer a differentiator
Lists 14 tools; positions Tabby and Continue at 33k+ GitHub stars each
Hybrid routing is the 2026 default: local Qwen for autocomplete, cloud for complex tasks
Qwen2.5-Coder became most-deployed self-hosted coding model in early 2026 (RunPod data, March 2026)
GDPR/Schrems II framing: US-hosted AI tools are active legal risk for EU fintech and healthcare
European VPS alternative: DD Large (16 vCPU/32GB RAM) at €49.99/month covers unlimited devs
Break-even vs Copilot at just 3-4 developers for European self-hosted setup
Annual saving at 50 devs: ~$10,490/yr vs GitHub Copilot
CPU inference is NOT viable for real-time FIM: returns completions in 1-2s vs 200-300ms budget
9 tools compared; Tabby framed as 'the path to getting AI coding tools approved by security teams'
GitHub Copilot usage-based billing starting June 1, 2026 cited as migration trigger
Copilot Enterprise: $39/user/month
Void not listed as a primary tool — Zed positioned as open-source Cursor/Void alternative
Kilo Code: 1.5M+ users; raised $8M seed in early 2026
Step-by-step VS Code + Ollama + Continue setup guide (March 2026)
Dual-model strategy: 7B for chat, 1.5B for autocomplete (speed-critical)
Setup time: roughly 30 minutes from a fresh start to a working setup
Electricity cost only ~$2-9/month for local inference
Hybrid recommended: local + pay-as-you-go cloud API for complex tasks
CLOUD Act and FISA Section 702 create irreconcilable conflict with GDPR Article 48
Microsoft admitted it cannot guarantee EU data inaccessibility to US government requests
GitHub Copilot and Claude Code flagged as US jurisdiction risk; local alternatives rated compliant by design
EU AI Act (effective August 2024): most coding agents classified as limited risk but with transparency obligations
Self-hosted: organization controls both provider and deployer roles under EU AI Act
Void positioned as 'open-source Cursor alternative for privacy absolutists'
Void development explicitly paused as of early 2026 — team exploring new direction
WARNING in GitHub README: 'we might not resume Void as an IDE'
Zed positioned separately from Void: speed-first, Rust-based, non-Electron
Void has 28.8k GitHub stars despite development pause

stats.fact: Over 60% of Fortune 500 companies have deployed AI coding assistants (90% of Fortune 100) as of 2026, yet 38% have experienced security incidents related to these tools
stats.source: Digital Applied, AI Coding Assistants Security: Best Practices Guide, January 2026

stats.fact: Developers report 40-55% faster code completion with AI coding assistants
Tabby GitHub stars: 33,500+; Continue GitHub stars: 33,000+; Void GitHub stars: 28,800 (development paused)
Qwen2.5-Coder 32B HumanEval: 92.7% (pass@1 instruct) vs GitHub Copilot Business ~75% estimated
stats.source: Qwen technical report; Spheron Network, April 2026

stats.fact: Break-even for self-hosted Tabby on A100 80GB ($749/month) vs GitHub Copilot ($19/seat) is at 39 developers
Self-hosted TTFT: Tabby + Qwen2.5-Coder 7B (L40S) = 80-200ms; Tabby + Qwen32B (A100) = 150-400ms; GitHub Copilot = 80-150ms
CPU-only inference (7B Q4_K_M, 16 vCPU): 8-15 tok/s — adequate for chat, NOT viable for inline FIM autocomplete (1-2s latency vs 200-300ms budget)
Qwen2.5-Coder became the most-deployed self-hosted coding model in early 2026, overtaking Llama-based models
GitHub Copilot usage-based billing starts June 1, 2026; Copilot Enterprise raised to $39/user/month
stats.source: GitHub documentation, cited by opensourcealternatives.to, April 2026

stats.fact: A single A100 80GB running Qwen2.5-Coder 32B (4-bit, ~20GB VRAM) handles 15-25 concurrent developers with Tabby's request queuing
European self-hosted alternative: €69.98/month (VPS + Postgres) for unlimited devs — break-even vs Copilot at 3-4 developers
Slopsquatting hallucination rate: commercial models ~5% hallucinated packages; open-source models ~22%
Major financial firm security incident: proprietary trading algorithms sent to AI assistant with default settings; estimated $12M remediation and legal fees
Ollama + Continue setup cost: $0/month for local inference; electricity ~$2-9/month depending on hardware
Void last meaningful source code activity: mid-2025; official pause notice visible on both GitHub and voideditor.com as of May 2026

angles: Void is effectively paused — the article should be honest that this 'contender' may not resume as an IDE, making it a risky dependency in 2026
The CPU vs GPU divide for FIM autocomplete is the central honest insight competitors avoid: CPU-only is NOT viable for real-time tab completion
GDPR and FISA/CLOUD Act conflict drives European enterprise self-hosting demand — US-hosted AI tools carry real legal risk for EU companies
Tabby wins for compliance-constrained regulated industries (healthcare, finance, defense, government) where Copilot cannot pass security review
Continue + Ollama wins for zero-cost individual devs and small teams under ~39 people
GitHub Copilot usage-based billing starting June 1, 2026 creates a natural reevaluation moment for self-hosted alternatives
Hybrid model routing is the dominant 2026 pattern: local Qwen for autocomplete + cloud API for complex tasks — tools that force all-or-nothing lose
'Supports Ollama' is now table-stakes, not a differentiator — the real differentiator is model routing, agent orchestration, and team management
The Three Flavors framing is useful: (1) local-first data (cloud model, local session), (2) local execution (zero-egress, GPU required), (3) self-hosted server (org controls infrastructure)
Self-hosted models on Qwen2.5-Coder 32B now EXCEED GitHub Copilot on HumanEval benchmarks (92.7% vs ~75%) but TTFT latency gap remains real
outline: H2: Why Developers Are Going Self-Hosted in 2026 (pricing changes, privacy laws, IP protection)
H2: The Three Flavors of Local AI: Know What You're Actually Choosing
H2: Tabby — The Full-Stack Team Solution
H2: Continue + Ollama — Zero-Cost Stack for Individuals and Small Teams
H2: Void — The Open-Source Editor That Hit Pause
H2: The CPU vs. GPU Reality Check (why not all self-hosted is equal)
H2: Head-to-Head Comparison: Cost, Latency, Privacy, and Team Features
H2: Which Tool Should You Choose? (decision framework by use case)
